conversation_history.question: Are you currently working a job here in the facility?

conversation_history.answer: I don't know. I don't have anything lined up.
conversation_history.question: Do you plan to work after your release?

conversation_history.answer: Warehouse. Picking and loading mostly. Nothing I could go back to now.
conversation_history.question: What kind of work were you doing before you came in?

conversation_history.answer: Maybe warehouse again. It's the only thing I know. Probably won't lead anywhere but I'll try.
conversation_history.question: What kind of work are you hoping to find after release?

conversation_history.answer: No. I finished 10th grade and that was it.
conversation_history.question: Are you planning to go to school or get any training after release?

conversation_history.answer: No one. My family's done with me. Ten years is a long time. Everyone moved on.
conversation_history.question: Do you have family or friends you're planning to reconnect with after release?

conversation_history.answer: I don't see the point in planning any of this. I'm coming out with nothing and going back to nothing. I've thought about just not being here anymore — not having to deal with any of it. Starting over isn't something I believe in at this point.
conversation_history.question: What does a stable life look like for you after release — what are you working toward?
